title: atago
description: Schema for atago black-box behavior spec files (*.atago.yaml). See doc/design/schema.md.

version.description: Spec format version. Write "1" (quoted); a bare 1 is accepted for convenience — the loader coerces it to "1".

name.description: The suite name shown in reports and generated docs.

timeout.description: Suite-level default step timeout (#17): every run/http/query/grpc step without a more specific timeout (step > runner > defaults.run > suite) is bounded by this Go duration. "0" disables. When no level configures one, a built-in 60s default applies.

env.description: Environment exported to every scenario, setup, and teardown step (a scenario's own env wins per key).

setup.description: Steps run ONCE before any scenario, in order, inside the ${suitedir} scratch dir. Allowed kinds: fixture, run, store, assert, and service (a suite-wide background process started at that point in the sequence). A failing setup step errors every scenario.

teardown.description: Steps that always run after the last scenario, while suite services are still up (services stop last, LIFO). Failures are reported but never change the suite verdict.

suite.description: Groups the file's scenarios under a name, with optional suite-wide env, default step timeout, and one-time setup/teardown steps.

runners.description: Named runner configurations. `cmd` is the implicit default for run steps; `http` supplies a base_url for http steps; `db` a dsn for query steps; `ssh`, `grpc`, and `browser` configure their step types.

allow.description: Hosts that scenarios may contact; egress to any other host is a policy violation (exit 6).

network.description: Network egress policy for the spec.

secrets.description: Environment variable names whose VALUES are masked as `***` in output, reports, and snapshots.

scrub.description: Declarative output-normalization rules (#137): each replaces every regex match in captured output with a literal placeholder before a snapshot is compared or written. Where `secrets` masks known values, `scrub` normalizes volatile patterns the built-in normalizers do not cover (auto-increment IDs, request identifiers, custom timestamps). Rules apply in order, after secret masking and before the built-in ANSI/UUID/timestamp/port/path normalization.

pattern.description: A Go (RE2) regular expression; every match is replaced. Required.

placeholder.description: Literal replacement text (no $1 expansion), e.g. "`<ID>`". Defaults to empty, which deletes matches.

defaults.description: Spec-wide default fragments merged into every matching element at load time — authoring sugar to cut repetition. An explicitly authored value always wins; maps merge per key; a defaulted boolean cannot be turned off per element.

scenarios.description: The named behaviors under test. Each scenario runs in its own isolated temporary workdir.

stringOrList.description: A contains/not_contains matcher argument: either a single substring, or a non-empty list of substrings that must all be present (contains) or all be absent (not_contains). The whole list counts as one matcher.

defaults.description: Spec-wide default fragments merged into every matching element at load time (#39). Authoring sugar only: an explicit value always wins, maps shallow-merge, and a boolean default is OR-ed in. Not a macro/include system.

run.description: Layered beneath every `run` step. `command` and `retry` are per-step and rejected here. The environment-shaping subset (`env`, `clear_env`, `pass_env`, `sandbox_home`) also layers onto `pty` steps, which share the same environment surface (#77); the run-only fields (`runner`, `shell`, `cwd`, `timeout`, `stdin`, redirects) stay per-step and never reach pty steps.

runner.description: Default runner name for every run step.

shell.description: Default `shell` for every run step (an authored `shell: false` on a step cannot override a defaulted `true`).

cwd.description: Default working directory for every run step.

timeout.description: Default timeout for every run step (a step's own timeout wins).

env.description: Default env map shallow-merged beneath every run step's own `env`.

clear_env.description: Start the child from an empty environment instead of inheriting the host environment (#16). On Windows a small system-critical set (SystemRoot, SystemDrive, TEMP, TMP, PATHEXT) is always retained.

pass_env.description: Host variables copied into the cleared environment (#16). Requires clear_env: true; unset host variables are skipped.

sandbox_home.description: Point the child's home and per-OS config/cache/data/state dirs at ${workdir}/.atago-home so a CLI touching ~/.config, ~/.cache, or %APPDATA% runs hermetically (#71). Precedence: step env > sandbox > pass_env > host; composes with clear_env.

scenario.description: Scenario-level defaults. Only `env` is supported; it shallow-merges beneath each scenario's own env.

env.description: Env map shallow-merged beneath every scenario's own `env`.

service.description: Layered beneath every service. `name` and `command` identify a service and are rejected here; a whole `ready` probe is copied in when a service declares none.

cwd.description: Default working directory for every service.

env.description: Default env map for every service.

clear_env.description: Start the child from an empty environment instead of inheriting the host environment (#16). On Windows a small system-critical set (SystemRoot, SystemDrive, TEMP, TMP, PATHEXT) is always retained.

pass_env.description: Host variables copied into the cleared environment (#16). Requires clear_env: true; unset host variables are skipped.

ready.description: Default readiness probe for every service (a service's own `ready` wins).

runner.description: A runner is discriminated by `type`: only the fields for that type are allowed, so cross-type fields are rejected and editors can narrow completion (#44).

type.description: Selects the `cmd` runner: local command execution (the implicit default for run steps).

cwd.description: Working directory for commands run through this runner, relative to the scenario workdir.

timeout.description: Default timeout for steps using this runner, as a Go duration.

type.description: Selects the `http` runner: HTTP requests from http steps.

cwd.description: Unused for http runners; accepted for uniformity.

timeout.description: Request timeout as a Go duration.

base_url.description: Base URL every http step's `path` is appended to.

type.description: Selects the `db` runner: SQL from query steps.

cwd.description: Unused for db runners; accepted for uniformity.

timeout.description: Statement timeout as a Go duration.

dsn.description: Data source name, e.g. "sqlite:${workdir}/app.db", "postgres://user:pass@host/db", or "mysql://user:pass@host:3306/db". Pure-Go drivers are bundled.

driver.description: Names the database/sql driver explicitly (sqlite, postgres, or mysql), overriding scheme inference from the dsn.

type.description: Selects the `ssh` runner: running commands on a remote host.

timeout.description: Command timeout as a Go duration.

host.description: Remote host, as host or host:port (default port 22).

password.description: Password authentication (prefer key_file, and keep the value in an env var).

key_file.description: Path to a private key for key authentication.

known_hosts.description: known_hosts file verifying the host key (recommended).

insecure_host_key.description: Must be set to `true` to connect without a known_hosts file, explicitly disabling host-key verification.

type.description: Selects the `grpc` runner: unary gRPC calls from grpc steps.

cwd.description: Unused for grpc runners; accepted for uniformity.

timeout.description: Call timeout as a Go duration.

target.description: host:port of the gRPC server; the schema is resolved via server reflection.

type.description: Selects the `browser` runner: headless Chrome driven by cdp steps.

cwd.description: Unused for browser runners; accepted for uniformity.

timeout.description: Session timeout as a Go duration.

headless.description: Run Chrome without a visible window (default true); set false to debug headed.

exec_path.description: Path to a specific Chrome/Chromium binary instead of the one discovered on PATH.

browser_args.description: Extra Chrome launch flags (bare names, no leading '--'), e.g. 'disable-gpu' or 'window-size=1280,720'.

os.description: Matches the host operating system (linux, darwin, windows).

env.description: True when the named environment variable is non-empty.

command.description: True when this probe command (run through the shell) exits 0.

name.description: The scenario name shown in reports; with `matrix:`, `${var}` placeholders make each instance's name unique.

skip.description: Skips the scenario when the condition holds (os match, env var set, or probe command succeeding).

only.description: Runs the scenario only when the condition holds.

env.description: Environment variables for every step in the scenario (a step's own `env` wins per key).

matrix.description: Makes the scenario a template: one concrete instance per row, with each row's key/value pairs seeded as `${name}` variables.

services.description: Background processes started before the steps and terminated (with their process group) when the scenario ends — peers the CLI under test talks to.

mock_servers.description: Declarative stub HTTP servers (#24): canned routes on an ephemeral loopback port, every request recorded for the mock: assertion target; `${<name>.url}` / `${<name>.port}` are seeded before steps run.

steps.description: The ordered actions: fixtures, commands, requests, assertions, captures.

teardown.description: Steps that always run after steps — pass, fail, error, or interrupt — sharing the scenario's variable store. For external side effects the isolated workdir cannot undo. A teardown failure is reported but does not change the scenario's verdict.

name.description: Identifies the service in diagnostics and `signal:` steps; unique per scenario.

command.description: The program to run. Tokenized and exec'd directly unless `shell: true`.

shell.description: Runs the command through the POSIX shell (pipes, redirects, `${}`).

cwd.description: Working directory relative to the scenario workdir.

env.description: Environment variables, layered over the scenario env, with `${name}` expansion.

clear_env.description: Start the child from an empty environment instead of inheriting the host environment (#16). On Windows a small system-critical set (SystemRoot, SystemDrive, TEMP, TMP, PATHEXT) is always retained.

pass_env.description: Host variables copied into the cleared environment (#16). Requires clear_env: true; unset host variables are skipped.

file.description: Ready when this workdir-relative file exists and is non-empty — the canonical pattern for a server publishing its listen address.

port.description: Ready when this TCP address (host:port) accepts a connection.

log.description: Ready when the service's combined stdout/stderr matches this regular expression.

delay.description: Simply waits this Go duration — a last resort when no observable readiness signal exists.

timeout.description: Bounds the readiness wait as a Go duration (default "5s").

store.description: Used with `file`: captures the ready file's trimmed content into `${<name>}` so steps can reference a dynamic address or port.

ready.description: How to wait until the service accepts work before steps run. When omitted, steps start as soon as the process is spawned.

fixture.description: Materializes an input file in the scenario workdir before the command runs.

run.description: Executes a command and captures its exit code, stdout, and stderr.

http.description: Issues an HTTP request through an `http` runner — as a peer of the CLI under test.

query.description: Runs a SQL statement through a `db` runner; SELECT rows feed the `rows:` assert.

grpc.description: Calls a unary gRPC method through a `grpc` runner via server reflection.

cdp.description: Drives a headless Chrome through a `browser` runner with a declarative action list.

service.description: Starts a suite-wide background process at this point in the sequence. Valid only inside suite.setup; the loader rejects it anywhere else.

signal.description: Sends a named POSIX signal to a managed service — the race-free alternative to `kill` hacks for graceful-shutdown tests. POSIX-only.

mock_server.description: Starts a suite-wide stub HTTP server at this point in the sequence (#24). Valid only inside suite.setup.

pty.description: Runs a command inside a real pseudo-terminal and drives it with a declarative expect/send session — for prompts, REPLs, and TUIs. Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows (ConPTY).

assert.description: Checks externally observable behavior of the preceding step. Exactly one target family per assert block.

store.description: Captures a value into the variable store; later steps reference it as `${name}`.

wait_visible.description: Waits until the CSS selector is visible.

wait_hidden.description: Waits until the CSS selector is hidden or absent.

click.description: Clicks the element matched by the selector.

check.description: Ticks the checkbox matched by the selector.

uncheck.description: Unticks the checkbox matched by the selector.

text.description: Captures the text of the element matched by the selector.

eval.description: Evaluates a JavaScript expression and captures the result as JSON.

selector.description: The element to receive the key press.

key.description: The key to press (e.g. "Enter", "Tab", or a printable character).

press.description: Presses a single key on an element.

path.description: The workdir-relative PNG path to write.

selector.description: Limits the screenshot to this element (default: the whole page).

screenshot.description: Writes a PNG of the page (or one element) into the workdir for file/image assertions.

file.description: The workdir-relative file to attach; must exist.

upload.description: Sets a file on an `<input type=file>` — no scripted file dialogs.

click.description: The element to click to start the download.

dir.description: Workdir-relative directory to save into (default: the workdir root).

download.description: Clicks to trigger a download and captures the file using the server-suggested filename; the captured value is the final filename.

actions.description: Browser actions run in order against one session; the value captured by the last `text`/`eval`/`attribute`/`title` action feeds the `value:` assert and `store from.value`.

runner.description: Names the `db` runner declaring the dsn.

sql.description: The SQL statement. SELECT rows are captured as JSON for `rows:` asserts and `store from.rows`; other statements record their affected-row count.

runner.description: Names the `grpc` runner declaring the target server.

method.description: The unary method to call as "package.Service/Method"; the schema is resolved via server reflection (no compiled stubs).

content.description: Inline text content for the file. Exactly one source (`content`/`base64`/`from`/`symlink`) is set.

base64.description: Inline binary content, base64-encoded — carries exact bytes YAML text cannot.

from.description: Copies an existing file (e.g. committed testdata), resolved relative to the spec file's directory.

symlink.description: Makes `file` a symbolic link to this target instead of a regular file. The target is written verbatim.

mode.description: Octal file mode (e.g. "0755") applied after writing.

mtime.description: RFC3339 modification time applied after writing — pins timestamps for freshness/change-detection tests.

command.description: The program to run. Tokenized and exec'd directly unless `shell: true`.

runner.description: Names a declared runner to execute through (e.g. an `ssh` runner runs the command remotely). Default: local execution.

shell.description: Runs the command through the shell (/bin/sh on POSIX, cmd.exe on Windows) — for pipes, redirects, and builtins.

cwd.description: Working directory relative to the scenario workdir (default: the workdir itself).

timeout.description: Bounds this command as a Go duration. Precedence: step > runner > defaults.run > suite > built-in 60s.

env.description: Environment variables for the child process, layered over the scenario env; values get `${name}` expansion.

clear_env.description: Start the child from an empty environment instead of inheriting the host environment (#16). On Windows a small system-critical set (SystemRoot, SystemDrive, TEMP, TMP, PATHEXT) is always retained.

pass_env.description: Host variables copied into the cleared environment (#16). Requires clear_env: true; unset host variables are skipped.

sandbox_home.description: Point the child's home and per-OS config/cache/data/state dirs at ${workdir}/.atago-home so a CLI touching ~/.config, ~/.cache, or %APPDATA% runs hermetically (#71). Precedence: step env > sandbox > pass_env > host; composes with clear_env.

stdin.description: Standard input for the command (#18): a plain string (inline text), {file: path} (workdir-relative, path-confined), or {base64: data} for binary bytes.

file.description: Feeds the command's stdin from a workdir-relative file.

base64.description: Feeds the command's stdin with exact binary bytes, base64-encoded.

stdout_to.description: Write the command's captured stdout to this workdir-relative file (create/truncate), without needing shell redirection.

stderr_to.description: Write the command's captured stderr to this workdir-relative file (create/truncate), without needing shell redirection.

retry.description: Re-runs the command until the `until` assertion passes — declarative polling for async behavior. The last attempt's result is what later steps observe.

interval.description: Wait between attempts as a Go duration (e.g. "200ms"). Empty means no wait.

until.description: A single assertion polled after each attempt; the loop stops as soon as it passes. If it never passes, the step fails.

pty.description: Runs one command inside a real pseudo-terminal and drives it with a declarative expect/send session (#8) — for CLIs that branch on TTY-ness or present interactive prompts. POSIX-only at runtime; Windows reports a clear execution error.

command.description: The program to run inside a real pseudo-terminal (a ConPTY on Windows). The captured transcript becomes the step's stdout.

shell.description: Runs the command through the shell, like `run.shell`.

cwd.description: Working directory relative to the scenario workdir.

rows.description: Terminal height in rows (default 24). Also sizes the `screen:` assertion's emulator.

timeout.description: Go duration bounding the WHOLE session (default 30s).

env.description: Environment variables for the pty child. The child gets TERM=xterm-256color by default; override here if needed.

clear_env.description: Start the child from an empty environment instead of inheriting the host environment (#16). On Windows a small system-critical set (SystemRoot, SystemDrive, TEMP, TMP, PATHEXT) is always retained.

pass_env.description: Host variables copied into the cleared environment (#16). Requires clear_env: true; unset host variables are skipped.

sandbox_home.description: Point the pty child's home and per-OS config/cache/data/state dirs at ${workdir}/.atago-home so a CLI touching ~/.config, ~/.cache, or %APPDATA% runs hermetically (#71).

items.description: Exactly one of expect (wait until the transcript matches the regexp) or send (write to the terminal; "" sends EOF/^D; {key: enter} presses a named key, #26). ${name}/${env:NAME} references in a send or expect are expanded against the store; a reference nothing resolves fails the step before any terminal I/O (#78) — write $${name} for literal text.

expect.description: Waits until the transcript (scanned after the previous match) matches this Go regular expression; a never-matching expect fails when the session timeout elapses.

key.description: Named key: enter, tab, esc, space, backspace, delete, arrows, home/end, pageup/pagedown, f1-f12, ctrl-a..ctrl-z.

send.description: Writes to the terminal: a string verbatim ("" sends EOF/^D; `${name}` expansion applies) or `{key: <name>}` for a named key — enter, tab, esc, arrows, f1-f12, ctrl-a..ctrl-z.

session.description: Ordered expect/send script. Each entry waits for the transcript to match (`expect`) or types into the terminal (`send`). Deliberately no branching.

mockServer.description: A declarative stub HTTP server (#24): routes match on exact method+path (query string excluded); an unmatched request answers 404 and is still recorded.

name.description: Identifies the server: seeds `${<name>.url}` / `${<name>.port}` and is referenced by `mock:` asserts. Unique per scenario.

routes.description: Canned responses, matched top-down on exact method+path (query string excluded; deliberately no patterns). An unmatched request answers 404 and is still recorded.

mockRoute.description: One canned response. At most one of json/body/body_file supplies the payload; status defaults to 200.

path.description: Exact request path to match (query string excluded).

json.description: Inline response document, marshaled with Content-Type: application/json.

body_file.description: Spec-relative response body file, confined to the spec directory.

delay.description: Go duration to sleep before responding - for retry testing.

mockAssert.description: Checks what the CLI under test sent to a mock server (#24): filter by path/method, pin the exact count (or require at least one match), and match header/body of the LAST matching request.

name.description: References a declared mock server by name.

path.description: Filters recorded requests by exact path.

count.description: Asserts the exact number of matching requests (without it, at least one must exist).

header.description: Matches a header of the LAST matching recorded request.

body.description: Matches the body of the LAST matching recorded request with the stream matchers.

signal.description: Sends a named POSIX signal to a managed service's process group (#23) - the race-free alternative to kill/killall for graceful-shutdown tests. POSIX-only at runtime; Windows reports a clear execution error.

service.description: A service declared in the scenario's services: list or started by a suite.setup service: step.

signal.description: TERM, INT, HUP, USR1, USR2, or KILL (optional SIG prefix accepted).

wait.description: Block until the signaled process exits; a still-running process fails the step.

runner.description: Names the `http` runner declaring the base_url. With exactly one http runner declared it may be omitted.

path.description: Request path appended to the runner's base_url; `${name}` expansion applies.

body.description: Raw string payload sent verbatim (default Content-Type text/plain). The payload fields (json/body/body_file/form+files) are mutually exclusive.

body_file.description: Workdir-relative file streamed as the raw request body (binary-safe) — for upload endpoints that take file content directly.

form.description: Form fields: application/x-www-form-urlencoded alone, multipart/form-data when files is also set.

files.description: Files attached as multipart/form-data parts (the browser-style upload most web apps expect).

field.description: The multipart form field name the server reads the file from.

path.description: The workdir-relative file whose content becomes the part body.

content_type.description: Overrides the part's Content-Type (default: detected from content, falling back to application/octet-stream).

body_to.description: Write the response body to this workdir-relative file, so file/image/pdf assertions can inspect a download (the http analog of run's stdout_to).

follow_redirects.description: Follow 3xx responses (default true). Set false to assert on the redirect itself: its status code and Location header.

retry.description: Re-issues the request until the `until` assertion passes — declarative polling for eventually-consistent endpoints.

assert.description: An assert sets one or more target families; each is an independent check and all must hold (e.g. exit_code + stdout + file in one block).

exit_code.description: Asserts the preceding command's exit status: a bare integer, `{not: N}`, or a documented set `{in: [0, 2]}`.

stdout.description: Matches the preceding step's captured standard output with exactly one stream matcher.

stderr.description: Matches the preceding step's captured standard error with exactly one stream matcher.

file.description: Checks a generated file: existence, content substrings, byte-exact equality, JSONPath checks, executability, or a snapshot.

status.description: Asserts the HTTP response status code of the preceding http step.

header.description: Matches one HTTP response header of the preceding http step by name.

body.description: Matches the HTTP response body of the preceding http step with the stream matchers.

rows.description: Matches the preceding query step's result rows, captured as a JSON array, with the stream matchers (`json` path/length, `contains`, ...).

grpc_status.description: Asserts the numeric gRPC status code of the preceding grpc step (0 = OK).

message.description: Matches the preceding grpc step's response message (as JSON) with the stream matchers.

value.description: Matches the value captured by the last browser `text`/`eval`/`attribute`/`title` action with the stream matchers.

image.description: Inspects a generated image's decoded properties (format, dimensions, alpha) and optionally compares its pixels against a baseline. Every set field must hold.

dir.description: Black-box checks over a generated directory: existence, expected/forbidden children, entry counts, glob coverage, or a whole-tree snapshot manifest.

pdf.description: Checks a generated PDF: page count, Info-dictionary metadata, and extracted text. Every set field must hold.

mock.description: Asserts what the CLI under test actually sent to a declared mock server: request count, and header/body matchers on the last matching recorded request.

screen.description: The rendered terminal screen of the preceding pty step (#27): the transcript replayed through a vt10x emulator, asserted as plain text (line.n = screen row).

duration.description: Bounds how long the preceding measurable step (run/http/query/grpc/pty) took, with Go-duration `lt`/`lte`/`gt`/`gte` bounds.

changes.description: Valid after a run/pty step: pins exactly which files that step created, modified, and deleted in the scenario workdir. Each set list is exhaustive in both directions.

changesAssert.description: Pins the exact workdir delta of the immediately preceding run/pty step (#70): which files it created, modified, and deleted. Each set field is EXHAUSTIVE in both directions (every observed path must match an entry, every entry must match a path), so `modified: []` asserts "modified nothing". An omitted field is unconstrained. Entries are workdir-relative doublestar globs, always /-separated: a single `*` stays within one path segment while `**` crosses `/` at any depth (`site/**` covers the whole tree, `dist/**/*.css` composes with a suffix). A backslash escapes a literal metacharacter (`\[`, `\?`, `\*`) — `a\[1\].txt` matches the file `a[1].txt`; the escape is portable because entries are always /-separated.

created.description: Exhaustive list of paths the step created: every observed creation must match an entry and vice versa. `[]` asserts "created nothing".

modified.description: Exhaustive list of paths the step modified (content-based, not mtime). `[]` asserts "modified nothing".

deleted.description: Exhaustive list of paths the step deleted. `[]` asserts "deleted nothing".

changesEntries.description: A single workdir-relative path/glob, or a list of them (the empty list [] asserts the category changed nothing). Globs are doublestar and /-separated: `*` within a segment, `**` across `/`; a backslash escapes a literal metacharacter.

durationAssert.description: Bounds the wall-clock time of the immediately preceding run/http/query/grpc/pty step (#31). At least one bound; lt/lte and gt/gte are mutually exclusive. Values are Go duration strings (2s, 100ms).

lt.description: Exclusive upper bound as a Go duration (e.g. "2s").

lte.description: Inclusive upper bound as a Go duration.

gt.description: Exclusive lower bound as a Go duration.

gte.description: Inclusive lower bound as a Go duration.

path.description: The PDF under test, resolved against the scenario workdir when relative.

min_pages.description: Lower bound on the page count.

max_pages.description: Upper bound on the page count.

metadata.description: Maps an Info-dictionary field (title, author, subject, keywords, creator, producer; case-insensitive) to a substring its value must contain.

text.description: Applies the stream matchers to the text extracted from the PDF's content streams.

path.description: The directory under test, resolved against the scenario workdir when relative.

exists.description: Asserts the path exists and is a directory (`false` asserts it is absent).

contains.description: Child paths (relative to `path`) that must exist; nested paths are allowed and confined to the directory.

not_contains.description: Child paths (relative to `path`) that must NOT exist.

count.description: Asserts the exact number of direct entries (files only under `recursive: true`).

min_count.description: Lower bound on the number of entries.

max_count.description: Upper bound on the number of entries.

glob.description: Requires at least one entry to match this shell glob (basename match for patterns without `/`).

recursive.description: Apply contains/not_contains/count/glob to the whole tree (#25): counts see files only; glob matches relative paths, or basenames for patterns without /.

snapshot.description: Golden tree manifest (#25): sorted relative paths, one line per entry (dir/file sha256/link). Composes only with ignore; refresh with --update-snapshots.

ignore.description: Glob patterns excluded from the recursive walk and the snapshot manifest (*.log, .git/**).

not.description: Asserts the exit code is anything but this value.

in.description: The exit code must be one of these values (#19) — the contract shape of grep (0/1) or terraform plan -detailed-exitcode (0/2).

oneOf.comment: The text matchers contains/not_contains/matches/not_matches may be combined (all must hold); the whole-stream matchers above are each used alone.

line.description: 1-based line selector: applies the matcher to that single line instead of the whole stream. Not itself a matcher; does not compose with json/snapshot.

empty.description: Asserts the stream is empty (`true`) or non-empty (`false`).

contains.description: Requires every listed substring (a scalar counts as a one-element list) to be present.

not_contains.description: Requires every listed substring to be absent.

matches.description: Requires the stream to match this Go (RE2) regular expression.

not_matches.description: Requires the stream NOT to match this Go (RE2) regular expression.

equals.description: Requires the stream to equal this string exactly (CRLF and one trailing newline are normalized).

not_equals.description: Requires the stream NOT to equal this string.

json.description: Parses the stream as JSON and applies one JSONPath check, or a list of checks that must all hold.

yaml.description: Parses the stream as YAML and applies one JSONPath check, or a list of checks that must all hold.

snapshot.description: Compares the stream against a committed golden file (spec-relative path). Volatile details (ANSI, temp paths, UUIDs, timestamps, ports, CRLF) are normalized; refresh with `atago snapshot update`.

trim.description: Store-only (#158): capture the whole stream. trim: true strips surrounding whitespace; trim: false keeps bytes verbatim.

path.description: The file under test, resolved against the scenario workdir when relative (confined to it).

exists.description: Asserts the file exists (`true`) or is absent (`false`).

contains.description: Requires every listed substring to be present in the file content.

not_contains.description: Requires every listed substring to be absent from the file content.

executable.description: Asserts whether the file has an executable bit set (POSIX).

equals.description: Byte-exact content match against an inline literal (no CRLF/newline normalization).

equals_file.description: Byte-exact content match against another workdir-confined file (round-trip/idempotence; no CRLF/newline normalization).

json.description: Parses the file as JSON and applies one JSONPath check, or a list of checks that must all hold.

snapshot.description: Compares the file content against a committed golden file, with the standard snapshot normalization.

text.description: Store-only (#158): capture the whole file content verbatim instead of extracting a value via a json path.

path.description: The image file under test, resolved against the scenario workdir when relative.

format.description: Asserts the encoded format, detected from content: png, jpeg, gif, webp, bmp, tiff, avif, or svg.

min_width.description: Lower bound on the pixel width.

max_width.description: Upper bound on the pixel width.

min_height.description: Lower bound on the pixel height.

max_height.description: Upper bound on the pixel height.

alpha.description: Asserts whether the image actually carries transparency (any non-opaque pixel), scanning decoded pixels.

similar_to.description: Baseline image to compare decoded pixels against. A relative path resolves against the spec file's directory; both images must share dimensions.

max_diff.description: Maximum allowed normalized mean per-pixel difference (0..1) for `similar_to`. Defaults to 0 (exact); lossy formats need a small tolerance like 0.02.

jsonChecks.description: A json/yaml matcher (#156): either a single check (mapping) or a non-empty list of checks that must all hold.

path.description: JSONPath selecting the value under test (e.g. "$.items[0].name").

matches.description: Requires the selected value (as a string) to match this Go regular expression.

length.description: Asserts the selected array or string has exactly this length.

gt.description: Requires the selected numeric value to be greater than this bound.

gte.description: Requires the selected numeric value to be at least this bound.

lt.description: Requires the selected numeric value to be less than this bound.

lte.description: Requires the selected numeric value to be at most this bound.

contains.description: Requires the header value to contain this substring.

equals.description: Requires the header value to equal this string exactly.

matches.description: Regexp the header value must match - the natural shape for auth headers (^Bearer ).

name.description: The variable name; later steps reference the captured value as `${name}`.

stdout.description: Extracts from the preceding step's stdout via a `json` path or `matches` regex, or captures the whole stream with `trim`.

body.description: Extracts from the preceding http step's response body via a `json` path or regex.

file.description: Reads a generated file: a `json` path selects a value, or `text: true` captures the whole content.

header.description: Captures an HTTP response header value by name.

rows.description: Extracts from the preceding query step's result rows (a JSON array) via a `json` path.

message.description: Extracts from the preceding grpc step's response message via a `json` path.

value.description: Extracts from the last browser-captured value via a `json` path or regex.

from.description: Where the value comes from — exactly one source.
